meta.description: The minimum complete grammatical unit. Domain + Action + Result, or variations. The most common form of everyday speech and inscription.

triads.interpretation: The beginning opens to the end. Strength passes through the threshold and finds its mark. The full arc of action — from origin, through passage, to seal.
triads.usage: The most common declarative triad. Inscribed on completed journeys, finished works, and successful passages. 'I came, I crossed, I am done.'
triads.grammarNotes: The canonical declarative. Aleph (domain) → Dalet (connector) → Taw (result). This is the pattern all other declarative triads vary from.

triads.interpretation: From the end, a passage back to the beginning. Completion opens a door to origin. The seal is not final — it returns.
triads.usage: Inscribed on cycles, returns, and rebirths. 'What is finished begins again.'
triads.grammarNotes: Inverted triad-001. Taw opens — time reverses. The inversive pattern used for prophecy and elegy.

triads.interpretation: Power enters the deep and finds a passage. Strength plunges into chaos and emerges with a threshold. The ox swims.
triads.usage: Inscribed on desperate journeys and passages through unknown territory. 'I went into the water and found the way.'
triads.grammarNotes: Mem inserted between domain and connector — deepens the passage. The door is not simply opened; it is found within chaos.

triads.interpretation: The passage leads through chaos to strength. To cross is to enter the deep and emerge powerful. The door that tests.
triads.usage: Inscribed on thresholds of initiation and trial. 'Pass through the water and become strong.'
triads.grammarNotes: Mem as the middle passage — the door opens onto chaos, but chaos leads to strength.

triads.interpretation: From the deep, strength opens a way. Chaos births power, and power makes a passage. The waters part.
triads.usage: A creation fragment. 'The deep gave strength, and strength made the door.' Inscribed on origin stories.
triads.grammarNotes: Two domains in sequence — Mem and Aleph both assert themselves before the connector. Rare. Weighty.

triads.interpretation: Make the passage and seal it. Build the door, then close it. The hand that opens also shuts.
triads.usage: Inscribed on finished constructions, locked doors, and sealed agreements. 'I made this passage. I seal it now.'
triads.grammarNotes: Imperative structure. Yod commands the making; Dalet is the passage made; Taw seals it.

triads.interpretation: The seal opens, and the hand passes through. What is finished becomes a threshold for action. The end enables making.
triads.usage: Inscribed on inherited tools, legacies, and traditions. 'The finished work of the dead opens the way for the living to make.'
triads.grammarNotes: Inverted triad-006. The seal opens, the door appears, the hand acts.

triads.interpretation: What holds opens to power. The structure contains a passage, and the passage leads to strength. Stability enables force.
triads.usage: Inscribed on fortified gates and defensive doors. 'The wall holds a door, and the door leads to strength.'
triads.grammarNotes: Samek as domain — the structure that permits passage, and passage that leads to power.

triads.interpretation: Power opens a passage to stability. The ox finds the door, and the door leads to what holds. Force finds structure.
triads.usage: Inscribed on sanctuaries reached through difficult paths. 'Fight through, and you will find refuge.'
triads.grammarNotes: The declarative arc ending in stability rather than completion. A comforting triad.

triads.interpretation: Cut away weakness, and strength opens the way. Divide, become strong, pass through.
triads.usage: Inscribed on weapons and tools of purification. 'Cleave the weak from the strong. The strong will open the door.'
triads.grammarNotes: Zayin in opening position — the cut commands. Aleph responds. Dalet is the result.

triads.interpretation: The passage empowers division. To cross is to become strong enough to cut. The door that arms those who enter.
triads.usage: Inscribed on armories and places of preparation. 'Pass through, gain strength, take up the blade.'
triads.grammarNotes: The door as origin — it is the passage that grants strength, and strength that enables the cut.

triads.interpretation: The deep shapes a passage. Chaos acts, and a door appears. The water itself builds the threshold.
triads.usage: Inscribed on natural passages, sea caves, and places where water has carved stone. 'The deep made this door.'
triads.grammarNotes: Mem as domain, Yod as action — the deep with hands. A creation fragment: chaos as builder.

triads.interpretation: The passage shapes the deep. The door acts upon chaos. To cross is to stir the waters.
triads.usage: A warning. 'Entering this door will disturb what lies beneath.' Inscribed on doors to vaults, tombs, and sealed depths.
triads.grammarNotes: The door as agent — it is the door that reaches into chaos.

triads.interpretation: Take up the blade and cut a passage. Make the weapon, use the weapon, open the way.
triads.usage: Inscribed on siege tools, pioneering equipment, and instruments of forced entry. 'Make your own door.'
triads.grammarNotes: Two actions in sequence — Yod makes the blade, Zayin cuts, Dalet is the passage made.

triads.interpretation: The passage divides the maker. The door cuts the hand that opens it. To cross is to be changed in your capacity to act.
triads.usage: A warning on transformative thresholds. 'You will not make as you made before.'
triads.grammarNotes: The door as agent of division — it cuts, and what is cut is the hand's ability.

triads.interpretation: Power divides the deep. Strength cuts chaos. The ox enters the water and parts it with its horns.
triads.usage: A creation fragment. 'Strength separated the waters.' Inscribed on cosmologies and origin texts.
triads.grammarNotes: Aleph opens, Zayin acts, Mem receives. The cut that creates by dividing chaos.

triads.interpretation: The deep divides and becomes strong. Chaos cuts itself and produces power. The water separates, and the ox emerges.
triads.usage: A cosmological fragment. 'The waters parted, and strength was born.'
triads.grammarNotes: Mem as domain of its own division — chaos as self-organizing. Aleph as the result of that division.

triads.interpretation: What holds stands in the deep and opens a passage. The pillar set in water — stability that creates a threshold even in chaos.
triads.usage: Inscribed on bridges, causeways, and foundations in water. 'The pillar in the flood makes the crossing.'
triads.grammarNotes: Samek as domain, Mem as the medium it stands in, Dalet as the passage it creates.

triads.interpretation: The passage leads through chaos to stability. Cross the water and find what holds. The door that promises refuge after the flood.
triads.usage: Inscribed on doors leading to sanctuaries across water. 'Pass through the deep. The pillar waits.'
triads.grammarNotes: Dalet opens, Mem is the passage through, Samek is the destination — stability after chaos.

triads.interpretation: From the end, strength opens a new passage. What is finished empowers a new beginning. The seal is not a wall — it is a door.
triads.usage: Inscribed on endings that are also beginnings. 'What you have finished gives you the strength to open the next door.'
triads.grammarNotes: Inversive. Taw opens, Aleph empowers, Dalet is the new passage.

triads.interpretation: Power acts to create structure. The ox builds. Strength, put to work, becomes what holds.
triads.usage: Inscribed on monuments, temples, and lasting works. 'Strength made this. It stands.'
triads.grammarNotes: Aleph opens, Yod acts, Samek receives. The transformation of force into lasting form.

triads.interpretation: The structure acts to empower. What holds shapes the hand, and the hand becomes strong. Stability teaches strength.
triads.usage: Inscribed on training grounds and places of learning. 'Stand here, work here, become strong.'
triads.grammarNotes: Samek as domain, Yod as action within that domain, Aleph as the result.

triads.interpretation: The deep opens to the seal. From chaos, through a passage, to completion. The water finds its door and is contained.
triads.usage: Inscribed on dams, levees, and waterworks. 'The flood is channeled. The deep is sealed.'
triads.grammarNotes: Mem opens, Dalet channels, Taw contains. The taming of chaos — a civilizing triad.

triads.interpretation: The seal breaks into chaos, and chaos opens a passage. What was contained is released, and the release makes a way.
triads.usage: A warning. 'Unsealing this will loose chaos — but chaos may open a door.' Inscribed on forbidden vaults.
triads.grammarNotes: Inversive. Taw opens — the seal breaks. Mem follows — chaos. Dalet is the unintended passage.

triads.interpretation: Build the structure and seal it. Make what holds, and finish the making. The builder's seal.
triads.usage: The canonical builder's inscription. 'I made this. It stands. It is done.' Placed on completed works.
triads.grammarNotes: Yod commands, Samek is what is built, Taw seals. Two result glyphs — the structure and its completion.

triads.interpretation: The sealed structure shapes the builder. What is finished and standing teaches the hand that made it. The work makes the worker.
triads.usage: Inscribed on masterworks that outlive their makers. 'What I finished, finished me.'
triads.grammarNotes: Inversive. The seal and the pillar precede the hand — the work creates the worker in retrospect.

triads.interpretation: Divide the deep and find power. Cut the waters, and what emerges is strong. The blade that births the ox.
triads.usage: A ritual creation fragment. 'Cut the water. Let strength rise.' Used in ceremonies of beginning.
triads.grammarNotes: Zayin commands the cut, Mem is what is cut, Aleph is what emerges.

triads.interpretation: Power enters the deep and acts. The ox wades into chaos and shapes it. Strength working within the formless.
triads.usage: Inscribed on works of art, music, and inspiration. 'Power drew form from the water.'
triads.grammarNotes: Aleph opens, Mem deepens, Yod acts within the deep. A creative triad — form drawn from formlessness.

triads.interpretation: Reach into the deep and pull out power. Shape the water, and what you shape will make you strong.
triads.usage: Inscribed on tools of art, craft, and inspiration. 'Make from the deep. What you make will make you.'
triads.grammarNotes: Yod commands the reaching, Mem is the medium, Aleph is the reward.

triads.interpretation: The structure and the power are both sealed. What holds and what drives are finished together. The world is built and strong and done.
triads.usage: Inscribed on completed worlds, finished eras, and final records. 'The pillar stands. The ox rests. It is finished.'
triads.grammarNotes: Two domains — Samek and Aleph — both sealed by Taw. A cosmological closing. Heavy and final.

triads.interpretation: From the seal, power builds structure. The end enables strength, and strength raises the pillar. What is finished becomes the foundation.
triads.usage: Inscribed on works built upon older works. 'The past is the ground. The ground holds the pillar.'
triads.grammarNotes: Inversive with two result positions — Taw opens and Samek closes. Aleph is the hinge.

triads.interpretation: The passage empowers the deep. To cross is to give strength to chaos. The door that feeds the water.
triads.usage: A dangerous inscription. 'Opening this door strengthens what lies beneath.' Used on seals and warnings.
triads.grammarNotes: Dalet opens, Aleph passes through, Mem is what receives the strength — and grows.

triads.interpretation: The deep and the power are sealed together. Chaos and strength, finished as one. The ox drowns, and the water is still.
triads.usage: Inscribed on tombs of heroes and monsters. 'The deep took the strong. Both are at rest.'
triads.grammarNotes: Two domains — Mem and Aleph — sealed by Taw. An elegiac triad. The end of struggle.

triads.interpretation: The seal contains the deep and produces power. What is finished holds chaos and draws strength from it. The sealed vessel empowers.
triads.usage: Inscribed on reliquaries, batteries, and containers of dangerous power. 'What is contained within gives strength.'
triads.grammarNotes: Inversive. Taw contains, Mem is contained, Aleph is the power drawn from containment.

triads.interpretation: Make strength, and it will open the way. Forge power, and the door appears. The hand creates the ox, and the ox finds the passage.
triads.usage: Inscribed on forges, training grounds, and places of preparation. 'Build your strength. It will open the door.'
triads.grammarNotes: Yod commands the making of strength; Aleph is the strength made; Dalet is the passage it opens.

triads.interpretation: The passage empowers action. The door gives strength, and strength reaches out. To cross is to become able.
triads.usage: Inscribed on doors to places of healing and restoration. 'Pass through. You will be strong enough to act.'
triads.grammarNotes: Dalet opens, Aleph strengthens, Yod acts — a restorative triad.

triads.interpretation: Cut the passage and seal it. Make the door with the blade, and close it behind you. The final passage.
triads.usage: Inscribed on doors that should not be reopened. 'I cut this way. I seal it now. Do not follow.'
triads.grammarNotes: Zayin cuts the passage, Dalet is the passage, Taw seals it. A triad of finality.

triads.interpretation: The seal opens a passage that divides. What is finished becomes a door, and the door is a blade. The end cuts.
triads.usage: Inscribed on endings that sever. 'What is done divides what was from what will be. You cannot go back.'
triads.grammarNotes: Inversive. Taw opens, Dalet is the passage, Zayin is what the passage does — it cuts.

triads.interpretation: The structure contains a passage, and the passage is sealed. The pillar's door is finished. The sanctuary is closed.
triads.usage: Inscribed on sealed sanctuaries and completed fortifications. 'The hold is built. The door is set. It is done.'
triads.grammarNotes: Samek opens, Dalet is within it, Taw seals. A triad of secure completion.

triads.interpretation: The seal becomes a structure, and the structure opens. What is finished stands, and in standing, offers passage.
triads.usage: Inscribed on ruins that have become doorways. 'The finished thing is now a threshold.'
triads.grammarNotes: Inversive. Taw is the origin, Samek is what it becomes, Dalet is what it offers.

triads.interpretation: The deep opens to power. Chaos contains a passage, and the passage leads to strength. The flood has a door, and the door leads to the ox.
triads.usage: Inscribed on places where danger leads to growth. 'Enter the water. Find the way. Emerge strong.'
triads.grammarNotes: Mem opens, Dalet is the passage within, Aleph is the result.

triads.interpretation: Power opens a passage to the deep. The ox finds the door, and the door leads into water. Strength chooses to enter chaos.
triads.usage: Inscribed on deliberate descents into danger. 'The strong one opened the door and entered the flood.'
triads.grammarNotes: Aleph opens, Dalet is the choice, Mem is the destination — a willing descent.

triads.interpretation: Make the seal, and let it stand. Finish the work, and let the work hold. The maker's command to the made thing.
triads.usage: Inscribed on works intended to outlast their makers. 'I made you finished. Now stand.'
triads.grammarNotes: Yod commands, Taw is the seal, Samek is the standing — two results, the second dependent on the first.

triads.interpretation: The structure, sealed, acts. What stands and is finished continues to shape. The monument makes.
triads.usage: Inscribed on monuments that inspire action. 'The finished pillar will move your hands.'
triads.grammarNotes: Samek opens, Taw seals, Yod acts from the sealed state — the monument as enduring agent.

triads.interpretation: Cut with the hand, and let it stand. Carve with your own grip, and what you carve will hold.
triads.usage: Inscribed on hand-carved works, sculptures, and inscriptions. 'Cut by hand. Let it stand.'
triads.grammarNotes: Two actions — Zayin cuts, Yod is the hand that guides the cut. Samek is the lasting result.

triads.interpretation: The structure guides the hand to cut. What holds teaches the hand to divide. Stability instructs the blade.
triads.usage: Inscribed on places of judgment and precision. 'Stand here. Let the pillar guide your cut.'
triads.grammarNotes: Samek as domain, Yod as the hand within that domain, Zayin as the action taken.

triads.interpretation: The deep, sealed, becomes power. Chaos contained produces strength. The tamed water drives the mill.
triads.usage: Inscribed on waterwheels, dams, and works that harness natural forces. 'The flood, channeled, turns the wheel.'
triads.grammarNotes: Mem opens, Taw contains, Aleph is the power drawn from containment.

triads.interpretation: Power, sealed, becomes the deep. Strength contained returns to chaos. The ox, penned, drowns in its own stillness.
triads.usage: A warning against stagnation. 'Strength unused becomes the flood that drowns it.'
triads.grammarNotes: Aleph opens, Taw contains, Mem is what containment produces — a dangerous stasis.

triads.interpretation: The passage, sealed, becomes power. A door that closes produces strength in its closing. The threshold that arms.
triads.usage: Inscribed on doors that empower those who close them. 'Shut this door, and be strong.'
triads.grammarNotes: Dalet opens, Taw seals, Aleph is the result of the sealing.

triads.interpretation: Power, finished, opens a passage. The ox, spent, becomes a threshold. What exhausts itself makes way for others.
triads.usage: Inscribed on memorials to those who gave everything. 'Their strength is spent. The door is open.'
triads.grammarNotes: Aleph opens, Taw closes Aleph's arc, Dalet is what remains — a passage for others.

triads.interpretation: The deep, the deep — and then a door. Chaos acknowledged in full, then a passage appears. The breath-mark before the threshold.
triads.usage: Inscribed on doors that appear only after the traveler has accepted the depth. 'Know the deep. Then the door.'
triads.grammarNotes: First appearance of punctuation in the lexicon. 𐤌𐤌 is a breath-mark — a pause, an intensification. The deep is not hurried past.

triads.interpretation: The pillar, the pillar — and it is done. The structure affirmed, then sealed. What stands, stands; now it is finished.
triads.usage: Inscribed on completed works that have stood for generations. 'It held. It holds. It is done.'
triads.grammarNotes: Samek doubled as breath-mark — the pillar acknowledged in its fullness before the seal.

triads.interpretation: Strength opens the door — the door, the door. The passage is doubled. The threshold is not one but many.
triads.usage: Inscribed on crossroads, junctions, and places of many choices. 'Power opens not one door but doors.'
triads.grammarNotes: Dalet doubled as breath-mark — the door multiplied. The passage is plural.

triads.interpretation: It is finished, it is finished — and from this, strength. The double seal produces power. The end, fully acknowledged, becomes a beginning.
triads.usage: Inscribed on endings that are celebrated, not mourned. 'It is done. It is done. Now: strength.'
triads.grammarNotes: Taw doubled as breath-mark — the seal intensified before Aleph emerges.

triads.interpretation: Take up the blade and cut the deep. The hand that wields the weapon against the water. The warrior enters the flood.
triads.usage: Inscribed on weapons meant for impossible battles. 'Strike the water. See what rises.'
triads.grammarNotes: Yod commands the grip, Zayin is the blade, Mem is what is struck.

triads.interpretation: The deep reaches up and cuts. Chaos has hands, and the hands hold blades. The water arms itself.
triads.usage: A warning. 'The deep will cut you.' Inscribed on places where the water is dangerous.
triads.grammarNotes: Mem as domain, Yod as the hand of the deep, Zayin as what that hand does.

triads.interpretation: The structure divides the deep. What holds cuts the water. The pillar set in the flood parts the current.
triads.usage: Inscribed on bridge piers, seawalls, and structures in water. 'The pillar stands. The water breaks.'
triads.grammarNotes: Samek opens, Zayin is the cut it makes, Mem is what is cut.

triads.interpretation: The deep divides and a pillar rises. Chaos cuts itself, and what emerges is structure. The water parts; the stone stands.
triads.usage: A creation fragment. 'The waters separated. The pillar appeared.' Inscribed on origin stones.
triads.grammarNotes: Mem divides itself, Zayin is the cut, Samek is what emerges — order from chaos.

triads.interpretation: Reach into the deep — the deep, the deep. The hand plunges, and the chaos is fully felt. An act of total immersion.
triads.usage: Inscribed on places of ritual immersion and baptism. 'Put your hand in. Feel all of it.'
triads.grammarNotes: Mem doubled as breath-mark — the deep experienced in full. The hand does not flinch.

triads.interpretation: Strength, strength — and then a door. Power fully gathered, then a passage opens. The ox draws a deep breath and charges.
triads.usage: Inscribed on places of muster and gathering. 'Gather your strength. Then the door.'
triads.grammarNotes: Aleph doubled as breath-mark — strength intensified before the passage.

triads.interpretation: The passage shapes the structure. The door acts upon the pillar. To cross is to change what holds.
triads.usage: Inscribed on doors that transform the building they belong to. 'This door changes the house.'
triads.grammarNotes: Dalet as agent, Yod as the action it takes, Samek as what is acted upon.

triads.interpretation: The structure opens a passage for the hand. What holds makes a door, and the door enables action. Stability permits making.
triads.usage: Inscribed on workshops within sanctuaries. 'The pillar holds. The door opens. Make.'
triads.grammarNotes: Samek holds, Dalet opens within the hold, Yod acts through the opening.

triads.interpretation: Carve the pillar, and it will give strength. Shape the stone, and the stone will empower. The sculptor's command.
triads.usage: Inscribed on quarries and carving sites. 'Cut the pillar. It will make you strong.'
triads.grammarNotes: Zayin carves, Samek is the pillar carved, Aleph is the strength gained.

triads.interpretation: Power and structure together divide. The ox and the pillar — combined, they cut. Strength channeled through stability.
triads.usage: Inscribed on fortifications and defensive works. 'The wall and the garrison — together they strike.'
triads.grammarNotes: Two domains — Aleph and Samek — before Zayin acts. Combined force.

triads.interpretation: The seal shapes the passage. What is finished acts to open the way. The done thing reaches out and makes a threshold.
triads.usage: Inscribed on works that continue to act after completion. 'The finished thing will open the door.'
triads.grammarNotes: Inversive. Taw opens, Yod acts from the sealed state, Dalet is what the action produces.

triads.interpretation: The passage seals the hand. The door closes on the maker. To cross is to finish what you were making.
triads.usage: Inscribed on doors that end a chapter of work. 'Pass through. The work is done. Your hands are still.'
triads.grammarNotes: Dalet opens, Taw seals the passage, Yod is what is sealed — the hand at rest.

triads.interpretation: The deep acts and is sealed. Chaos reaches out, and its reaching is completed. The water shapes, and the shape is finished.
triads.usage: Inscribed on works of art drawn from the unconscious. 'The deep made this. It is done.'
triads.grammarNotes: Mem opens, Yod is the hand of the deep, Taw seals the work.

triads.interpretation: The seal acts upon the deep. What is finished reaches into chaos. The done thing stirs the water.
triads.usage: Inscribed on works that disturb the old order. 'The finished thing will trouble the deep.'
triads.grammarNotes: Inversive. Taw opens, Yod acts, Mem is what is acted upon — the deep disturbed.

triads.interpretation: Make the final cut, and find power. Sever completely, and what remains is strong. The last division produces strength.
triads.usage: Inscribed on final partings and clean breaks. 'Cut it clean. End it. Be strong.'
triads.grammarNotes: Zayin commands the cut, Taw seals it, Aleph is what the sealed cut produces.

triads.interpretation: Power reaches completion — it is finished, finished. The ox has drawn the plow to the end of the field. The seal is doubled. The work is done, done.
triads.usage: The final triad. Inscribed on completions of great works, ends of eras, and the last line of inscriptions. 'It is finished. It is finished.'
triads.grammarNotes: Taw doubled as breath-mark, approaching seal-mark. The triad that ends the triads. The completion that acknowledges itself.
